mission.title: Install and Configure Hwameistor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Hwameistor is an HA local storage system designed for cloud-native stateful workloads. It enables the creation of a local storage resource pool for managing various disk types, providing data persistence capabilities for applications.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Hwameistor Helm chart repository to your local Helm installation with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add hwameistor https://hwameistor.io/helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Hwameistor using Helm
steps.description:
Install Hwameistor in a new namespace called 'hwameistor' with the following command:
```bash
helm install hwameistor hwameistor/hwameistor --namespace hwameistor --create-namespace --version 0.16.5
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Hwameistor pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace hwameistor
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Helm release to set resource limits for the Hwameistor components:
```bash
helm upgrade hwameistor hwameistor/hwameistor --namespace hwameistor --set scheduler.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set scheduler.resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Set up ServiceMonitor for monitoring
steps.description:
Apply the ServiceMonitor configuration to enable monitoring:
```bash
kubectl apply -f servicemonitor-example.yaml --namespace hwameistor
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Hwameistor pods running in the 'hwameistor' namespace, and you will see them listed when running `kubectl get pods --namespace hwameistor`. Additionally, the ServiceMonitor will be set up for monitoring.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add hwameistor https://hwameistor.io/helm
helm repo update
helm upgrade hwameistor hwameistor/hwameistor --namespace hwameistor --set scheduler.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set scheduler.resources.limits.memory=512Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Hwameistor Helm release to remove the deployment from the cluster. Use the following command to delete the release:
```bash
helm uninstall hwameistor --namespace hwameistor
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Hwameistor. Execute the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd hwameistorinstances.hwameistor.io
kubectl delete crd hwameistorvolumes.hwameistor.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace hwameistor
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'hwameistor' namespace to clean up all resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed by checking the list of namespaces:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace hwameistor
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of the Hwameistor installation. You can use the following command to export the current Helm release configuration:
```bash
helm get values hwameistor --namespace hwameistor > hwameistor-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Hwameistor release to the latest version. Use the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade hwameistor hwameistor/hwameistor --namespace hwameistor --version 0.16.6
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the Hwameistor pods are running with the new version. Check the status of the pods using:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace hwameistor
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Hwameistor pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for the specific pod to diagnose the issue:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace hwameistor
```

troubleshooting.title: Persistent volume claims not binding
troubleshooting.description:
If PVCs are not binding, ensure that the storage class is correctly configured and available. Check the PVC status with:
```bash
kubectl get pvc --namespace hwameistor
```

troubleshooting.title: ServiceMonitor not working
troubleshooting.description:
If the ServiceMonitor is not functioning, verify that the ServiceMonitor resource is correctly applied and check the logs of the monitoring tool:
```bash
kubectl get servicemonitor --namespace hwameistor
kubectl logs <monitoring-tool-pod-name> --namespace <monitoring-namespace>
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm upgrade fails due to a version conflict, ensure that the version specified is available in the Helm repository. You can list available versions with:
```bash
helm search repo hwameistor/hwameistor --versions
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
